skill.name: commit
skill.description: Create Sentry-style commit messages with conventional commit types, concise subjects, useful bodies, and issue references.
skill.summary: Format Git commit messages with Sentry conventions and issue references.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and flagged reference URLs as network use. Review found documentation-only Git examples, source links, and no executable script, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Manual Git Command Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill shows users Git commands for checking the current branch and creating a feature branch. This is expected for a commit workflow skill, but users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible, ordinary Git commands in Markdown code fences. The skill does not include scripts or hidden execution, so the residual risk is user operational error.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Code Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across formatting examples and inline Markdown. These lines are prose, tables, placeholders, and commit message examples, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a Markdown instruction file. The flagged backticks delimit text examples and do not create an execution path.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Without Active Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The skill includes a source URL and a documentation reference URL. No evidence found of HTTP requests, webhooks, download commands, or credential transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URL occurrences are plain Markdown metadata or references. They do not invoke network APIs or instruct automatic network access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on metadata and branch-formatting lines. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain description text, a Git branch command, and naming examples. They contain no cryptographic implementation or security-sensitive hashing.

content.value_statement: Teams need commit messages that are clear, reviewable, and linked to the right issues. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code format commits using Sentry conventions.
actual_capabilities: Formats commit headers with type, optional scope, and subject.
Explains accepted commit types for features, fixes, refactors, tests, and maintenance.
Guides issue footer usage for Fixes and Refs references.
Adds the Claude co-author footer when changes were primarily AI-generated.
Provides examples for fixes, features, refactors, breaking changes, and reverts.
limitations: Does not inspect repository diffs unless the user provides them or the agent reads them separately.
Does not validate issue IDs against Sentry, GitHub, or Linear.
Does not run Git commands automatically.
Uses Sentry-specific conventions that may not match every repository.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Clean Feature Commit
use_cases.description: Create a commit message with a feature type, optional scope, useful body, and linked issue.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Commit History
use_cases.description: Apply one message format across fixes, refactors, tests, and maintenance work.

use_cases.title: Document AI-Assisted Changes
use_cases.description: Add the required co-author attribution without adding unsupported AI labels.

prompt_templates.title: Format a Simple Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Sentry-style commit message for this change: fixed a null response crash in the user endpoint. Link it to SENTRY-5678.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changes and choose the correct Sentry commit type. Then write the subject, body, and issue footer.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an AI-Assisted Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Sentry-style commit message for these Claude Code changes. Include the proper co-author footer only if it applies.

prompt_templates.title: Split Complex Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this mixed diff and propose separate Sentry-style commits. Give each commit a type, scope, body, and issue reference.

output_examples.input: Fix a deleted-account crash in the dashboard and close SENTRY-5678.
output_examples.output:
fix(dashboard): Handle deleted account responses

The dashboard could crash when the user API returned a deleted account. Add a null check before reading account fields.

Fixes SENTRY-5678

output_examples.input: Add Slack thread replies for alert updates and reference GH-1234.
output_examples.output:
feat(alerts): Add Slack thread replies for alert updates

Post alert updates to the original Slack thread instead of creating a new message. This keeps related notifications grouped together.

Refs GH-1234

output_examples.input: Revert a feature commit because it caused a production regression.
output_examples.output:
revert: feat(api): Add new endpoint

This reverts the selected feature commit.

Reason: Caused performance regression in production.

best_practices: Keep each commit focused on one stable, reviewable change.
Use imperative present tense and keep the subject under 70 characters.
Reference issues in the footer with Fixes when the commit should close them.
anti_patterns: Do not include generated-by-AI phrases in the subject or body.
Do not combine unrelated fixes, features, and refactors in one commit.
Do not use vague subjects such as update files or fix stuff.

faq.question: What commit format does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses Sentry conventions based on conventional commit types, optional scopes, concise subjects, bodies, and footers.

faq.question: Does the skill run Git commands?
faq.answer: No. It documents normal Git commands and commit formats, but it does not execute commands by itself.

faq.question: Can it add issue references?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Fixes and Refs footers for GitHub, Sentry, and Linear-style issue IDs.

faq.question: When should I include the Claude co-author footer?
faq.answer: Include it when changes were primarily generated by a coding agent such as Claude Code.

faq.question: Can I use this outside Sentry projects?
faq.answer: Yes, but confirm that your repository accepts Sentry-style types, footers, and attribution rules.

faq.question: Does it validate that an issue exists?
faq.answer: No. It formats issue references, but it does not check external issue trackers.
